58	set_option autoImplicit false
59	set_option linter.style.setOption false
60	
61	universe u
62	
63	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
64	
65	noncomputable section
66	
67	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
68	
69	/-! ### (A) The projective line `ℙ¹` over `Spec k̄` (the `Proj` realisation) -/
70	
71	/-- The **standard ℕ-grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`** by total degree.
72	
73	This is the homogeneous-component decomposition `MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule`
74	specialised to two variables (`Fin 2`) over `k̄`. The `GradedRing` instance is
75	`MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra` (free).
76	
77	Used to define `ProjectiveLineBar = Proj` of this graded ring. -/
78	abbrev projectiveLineBarGrading (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
79	    ℕ → Submodule kbar (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
80	  MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar
81	
82	instance projectiveLineBarGrading_gradedRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
83	    GradedRing (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) :=
84	  MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra
85	
86	/-- **The projective line over `Spec k̄` as a scheme.** This is `Proj 𝒜` of the standard
87	ℕ-graded `k̄[X₀, X₁]`. Carries an `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance via
88	`Proj.toSpecZero` composed with the algebra-map identification of the degree-`0` piece
89	with `k̄` (Mathlib's `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebraSubtypeMemOfNat`).
90	
91	`IsProper` is FREE — see the `instIsProper` instance below. `IsAlgClosed kbar` is *not*
92	required for the scheme itself but is needed for downstream `Smooth`/geometric
93	irreducibility instances. -/
94	def ProjectiveLineBarScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
95	  Proj (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
96	
97	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` structure on `ProjectiveLineBarScheme` via
98	`Proj.toSpecZero` and the identification `k̄ ≃ ↥(𝒜 0)`. -/
99	instance projectiveLineBarScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
100	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
101	  hom := Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
102	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
103	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0)))
104	
105	/-- **The projective line `ℙ¹_{k̄}` as an object of `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** This is the
106	concrete scheme used by `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (iter-166) to formalise the
107	`𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut. -/
108	def ProjectiveLineBar (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
109	  (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
110	
111	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is proper over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
112	`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero…` — the algebra `k̄[X₀, X₁]` is finite type
113	over its degree-`0` piece `↥(𝒜 0) ≃ k̄` — chained via the standard properness-of-composition
114	lemma with the `Spec.map` of the algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` (which is bijective hence
115	gives an iso of `Spec`s).
116	
117	The chain:
118	* `Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜` is proper (Mathlib's
119	  `Proj.instIsProperToSpecZeroOfFiniteTypeSubtypeMemOfNatNat`, given
120	  `Algebra.FiniteType ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄)` — supplied by the
121	  `IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ _)` + `Algebra.FiniteType k̄ (MvPolynomial _ _)`
122	  chain via `Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType`);
123	* `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is an iso because `algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective
124	  in this standard ℕ-grading case (`𝒜 0 = MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) k̄ 0`
125	  is the constants subalgebra, naturally ≅ k̄);
126	* composition of proper + iso is proper. -/
127	instance projectiveLineBar_isProper (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
128	    IsProper (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom := by
129	  -- Unfold to expose the Proj.toSpecZero ≫ Spec.map chain.
130	  change IsProper (Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
131	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
132	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))))
133	  -- The IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ kbar) needed for the FiniteType derivation.
134	  haveI : IsScalarTower kbar
135	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
136	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
137	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
138	  -- MvPolynomial.{Fin 2} k̄ is finite type over ↥(𝒜 0) (chained from finite-type over k̄).
139	  haveI : Algebra.FiniteType
140	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
141	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
142	    Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType kbar _ _
143	  -- The algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective: the degree-`0` piece of the homogeneous
144	  -- decomposition is exactly the constants `C(k̄) ⊆ MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`. Injectivity
145	  -- follows from `MvPolynomial.C_injective`. Surjectivity uses
146	  -- `MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem` (which says the degree-`0` component of a
147	  -- homogeneous-degree-`0` polynomial is itself) plus `homogeneousComponent_zero`
148	  -- (which expresses the degree-`0` component as `C` of the constant coefficient).
149	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap kbar
150	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)) := by
151	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
152	    · intro x y h
153	      apply MvPolynomial.C_injective (Fin 2) kbar
154	      exact congrArg Subtype.val h
155	    · intro ⟨v, hv⟩
156	      refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v, ?_⟩
157	      apply Subtype.ext
158	      rw [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap]
159	      have key := MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)
160	      -- `key : (homogeneousComponent 0) v = if 0 = 0 then v else 0` which simp resolves
161	      -- and combines with `homogeneousComponent_zero` to give `C (coeff 0 v) = v`.
162	      simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true] at key
163	      exact key
164	  -- Therefore `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is iso, and composition of proper + iso is
165	  -- proper.
166	  haveI : IsIso (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
167	      (algebraMap kbar ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)))) := by
168	    rw [isIso_SpecMap_iff]
169	    exact hbij
170	  infer_instance
171	
172	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is geometrically irreducible over `Spec k̄`.** Project-side scaffold sorry
173	(Mathlib does not ship `GeometricallyIrreducible` for `Proj` of a polynomial ring;
174	plan-marked acceptable for iter-165). -/
175	instance projectiveLineBar_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
176	    GeometricallyIrreducible (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=
177	  sorry
178	
179	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is smooth of relative dimension `1` over `Spec k̄`.** Project-side scaffold
180	sorry (Mathlib does not ship `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` for `Proj`; plan-marked
181	acceptable for iter-165). -/
182	instance projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
183	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=
184	  sorry
185	
186	/-! ### The standard `k̄`-points `0`, `1`, `∞` on `ℙ¹`
187	
188	The three distinguished `k̄`-points of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` are `[0 : 1]` (the affine origin), `[1 : 1]`
189	(the affine unit), and `[1 : 0]` (the point at infinity). They are encoded as morphisms
190	`𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar`, i.e. sections of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom`.
191	
192	The construction goes through `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`: a `k̄`-point of `Proj 𝒜` is
193	specified by an evaluation ring map `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* k̄` (composed into
194	`Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`) whose image of the irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁)`
195	generates the unit ideal — automatic whenever the evaluation vector has a unit coordinate. -/
196	
197	/-- **The underlying ring map of a `k̄`-point of `ℙ¹`.** Sends `X₀ ↦ v 0`, `X₁ ↦ v 1`,
198	then composes back into `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via the inverse of `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. -/
199	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
200	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) :
201	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤) :=
202	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of kbar)).inv.hom.comp (MvPolynomial.eval v)
203	
204	/-- **Irrelevant-ideal-maps-to-top condition** for `ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal`: if one
205	of the coordinates `v 0` or `v 1` is a unit, then the image of `(X₀, X₁)` generates the
206	unit ideal in `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)`. -/
207	private lemma ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top
208	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
209	    Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
210	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal = ⊤ := by
211	  have hX_mem : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
212	      (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
213	    HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_of_mem _ Nat.zero_lt_one
214	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
215	  have hImg_mem : ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i) ∈
216	      Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
217	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
218	    Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ hX_mem
219	  have hImg_unit : IsUnit (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i)) := by
220	    unfold ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
221	    rw [RingHom.comp_apply, MvPolynomial.eval_X]
222	    exact hi.map _
223	  rw [Ideal.eq_top_iff_one]
224	  obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hImg_unit
225	  rw [show (1 : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)) = ((u⁻¹ : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)ˣ) : _) * u from
226	    (Units.inv_mul _).symm]
227	  exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (hu.symm ▸ hImg_mem)
228	
229	/-- **Helper: construct a `k̄`-point of `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`** from an evaluation vector
230	`v : Fin 2 → kbar` with at least one unit coordinate. The underlying scheme map is
231	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation; the section condition chases through
232	`fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` + `IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) MvPoly` collapse to
233	`MvPolynomial.C` + `MvPolynomial.eval_C` + `toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv`. -/
234	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec
235	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
236	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
237	  Over.homMk
238	    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
239	      (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
240	      (ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top kbar v i hi)) <| by
241	    -- Section condition: fromOfGlobalSections ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = 𝟙.
242	    haveI : IsScalarTower kbar ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
243	        (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
244	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
245	    -- Expose the structure morphism's composition shape.
246	    change Proj.fromOfGlobalSections _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
247	    -- Combine via `fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero`, then unify the two Spec.maps.
248	    rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc,
249	      ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
250	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
251	    -- The inner composition `(evalIntoGlobal v) ∘ C` collapses to `(ΓSpecIso).inv.hom`.
252	    -- Build a CommRingCat-level equation, then apply `Spec.map` and finish.
253	    have hcc : CommRingCat.ofHom
254	        ((ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v).comp MvPolynomial.C) =
255	        (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
256	      apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
257	      ext r
258	      change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom
259	          ((MvPolynomial.eval v) (MvPolynomial.C r)) = _
260	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval_C]
261	    calc _ = (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)).toSpecΓ ≫
262	            Spec.map (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
263	            exact congrArg _ (congrArg Spec.map hcc)
264	      _ = _ := AlgebraicGeometry.toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv _
265	
266	/-- The `k̄`-point `0 = [0 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` via
267	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation `X₀ ↦ 0`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
268	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
269	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
270	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 0 else 1) 1 (by simp)
271	
272	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 = [1 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
273	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
274	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
275	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
276	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun _ => 1) 0 (by simp)
277	
278	/-- The `k̄`-point `∞ = [1 : 0] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
279	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 0`. -/
280	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.inftyPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
281	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
282	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 1 else 0) 0 (by simp)
283	
284	/-! ### (B) The additive group `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` -/
285	
286	/-- **The additive group `𝔾_a = 𝔸¹` over `Spec k̄` as an underlying scheme.** This is the
287	affine line `AffineSpace (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))`. It is affine, locally of finite
288	presentation, and reduced (its global sections are `MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain). -/
289	def GaScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
290	  AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))
291	
292	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GaScheme` (via
293	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.over`). -/
294	instance gaScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
295	    (GaScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
296	  inferInstanceAs ((AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))).Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
297	
298	/-- **The additive group object `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
299	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
300	abbrev Ga (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
301	  (GaScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
302	
303	/-- **`𝔾_a` is an affine morphism over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
304	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.instIsAffineHomOverSchemeInferInstanceOverClass`. -/
305	instance ga_isAffineHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
306	    IsAffineHom (Ga kbar).hom :=
307	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffineHom (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘
308	    (Spec (.of kbar))))
309	
310	/-- **`𝔾_a` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
311	`AffineSpace.instLocallyOfFinitePresentation…OfFinite` (the index `Fin 1` is finite). -/
312	instance ga_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
313	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Ga kbar).hom :=
314	  inferInstanceAs (LocallyOfFinitePresentation
315	    (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘ Spec (.of kbar)))
316	
317	/-- **`𝔾_a`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** Since the global sections are
318	`MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain over a field, the affine scheme is reduced. The proof
319	transfers `IsReduced (Spec (.of (MvPolynomial _ _)))` (free for any reduced ring) across
320	`AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine`. -/
321	instance ga_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Ga kbar).left :=
322	  isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine (Fin 1) _).hom
323	
324	/-- **`GrpObj`-structure on `𝔾_a` via `ofRepresentableBy`.**
325	
326	Installs the additive-group structure on `Ga` using the Yoneda installer
327	`GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` (Mathlib's canonical mechanism) with the additive-group functor
328	`T ↦ AddGrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)` and the representable-by witness derived from
329	`AffineSpace.homOverEquiv`. Scaffold: full body is iter-166's lane (the homEquiv +
330	naturality glue is straightforward but needs careful unfolding).
331	
332	Scaffold body for iter-166+; off-path for the genus-0 closure (the rigidity consumer
333	`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` uses `W = Gm`, not `W = Ga` — `Ga` is on the demoted
334	additive route only). -/
335	instance ga_grpObj (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : GrpObj (Ga kbar) := sorry
336	
337	/-- **`𝔾_a` is smooth over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
338	`AlgebraicGeometry.smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed` once `GrpObj`, `LocallyOfFinitePresentation`,
339	and `IsReduced` are installed. -/
340	instance ga_smooth (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
341	    Smooth (Ga kbar).hom :=
342	  have : GrpObj (Over.mk (Ga kbar).hom) := ga_grpObj kbar
343	  smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed (Ga kbar).hom
344	
345	/-! ### (C) The multiplicative group `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` -/
346	
347	/-- **The ring `k̄[t, t⁻¹] = Localization.Away t`**, where `t = X () : MvPolynomial Unit k̄`.
348	This is the global-sections ring of `𝔾_m`. -/
349	abbrev GmRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Type u :=
350	  Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
351	
352	/-- **The multiplicative group `𝔾_m = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]` as an underlying scheme.** This is
353	the chosen affine encoding (the analogist `gm-scaling-p1` D2.b verdict: AFFINE `Spec`, NOT
354	the basic-open of `𝔸¹` — the latter loses `IsAffine`). -/
355	def GmScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
356	  Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))
357	
358	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GmScheme`. -/
359	instance gmScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
360	    (GmScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
361	  hom := Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))
362	
363	/-- **The multiplicative group object `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
364	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
365	abbrev Gm (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
366	  (GmScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
367	
368	/-- **`𝔾_m` is affine.** -/
369	instance gm_isAffine (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsAffine (Gm kbar).left :=
370	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffine (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
371	
372	/-- **`𝔾_m` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** Follows from
373	`Algebra.FinitePresentation k̄ (k̄[t, t⁻¹])` (a localization of a polynomial ring at a single
374	element is finitely presented), bridged through
375	`AlgebraicGeometry.HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff` for the `LocallyOfFinitePresentation` /
376	`RingHom.FinitePresentation` correspondence. -/
377	instance gm_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
378	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Gm kbar).hom :=
379	  (HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff (P := @LocallyOfFinitePresentation)).mpr
380	    ((RingHom.finitePresentation_algebraMap (B := GmRing kbar)).mpr
381	      inferInstance)
382	
383	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** `k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is a localization of a domain at
384	a non-zero-divisor, hence a domain, hence reduced. The `IsReduced (Spec _)` instance picks
385	up directly from `IsReduced (Localization.Away _)`. -/
386	instance gm_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Gm kbar).left :=
387	  inferInstanceAs (IsReduced (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
388	
389	/-- **`k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain.** Localization of the integral polynomial ring
390	`MvPolynomial Unit k̄` at the powers of a nonzero element `X () ≠ 0` preserves the domain
391	property (`IsLocalization.isDomain_localization` applied to
392	`Submonoid.powers (X ()) ≤ nonZeroDivisors`, the latter from `MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero`).
393	
394	Load-bearing for `gm_irreducibleSpace` and `gm_geomIrred`. -/
395	instance gmRing_isDomain (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsDomain (GmRing kbar) := by
396	  unfold GmRing
397	  exact IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
398	    (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors (MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero _))
399	
400	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is irreducible.** Follows from the fact that the
401	global-sections ring `GmRing = k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain (`gmRing_isDomain`)
402	together with Mathlib's `Spec` of a domain being irreducible
403	(`PrimeSpectrum.irreducibleSpace`). -/
404	instance gm_irreducibleSpace (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
405	    IrreducibleSpace (Gm kbar).left := by
406	  change IrreducibleSpace (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar)))
407	  infer_instance
408	
409	/-- **`GrpObj`-structure on `𝔾_m` via `ofRepresentableBy`.**
410	
411	Installs the multiplicative-group structure on `Gm` using `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with
412	the units functor `T ↦ GrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`. The representable-by witness exploits the
413	fact that morphisms into `Spec (Localization.Away t)` correspond exactly to units in the
414	global sections (Mathlib's `IsLocalization.Away`-Spec bijection). Scaffold body — same
415	discipline as `ga_grpObj`.
416	
417	This `GrpObj Gm` is the LIVE consumer of the iter-166 `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const`
418	proof body (the `𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut applies `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` with
419	`W = Gm`). -/
420	instance gm_grpObj (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : GrpObj (Gm kbar) := sorry
421	
422	/-- **`𝔾_m` is smooth over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from `smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed` once
423	`GrpObj`, `LocallyOfFinitePresentation`, and `IsReduced` are installed. -/
424	instance gm_smooth (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
425	    Smooth (Gm kbar).hom :=
426	  have : GrpObj (Over.mk (Gm kbar).hom) := gm_grpObj kbar
427	  smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed (Gm kbar).hom
428	
429	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 ∈ 𝔾_m` (the multiplicative identity), encoded as the group-object
430	unit `η[Gm kbar] : 𝟙_ ⟶ Gm kbar`. -/
431	def Gm.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
432	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Gm kbar :=
433	  η[Gm kbar]
434	
435	/-! ### (D) The `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`
436	
437	`gmScalingP1` is a *bare* `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`-morphism (the analogist D3 verdict:
438	no `IsAction`/`MulAction`-style typeclass at scheme level — Mathlib has no such precedent;
439	the rigidity consumer needs only the bare morphism + the named fixed-point lemma).
440	
441	Chartwise definition: on `𝔸¹ × 𝔾_m` (target chart `D₊(X₀)` of `ℙ¹`), the morphism is
442	the polynomial map `(x, λ) ↦ λx`; near `∞` (target chart `D₊(X₁)`, coordinate `u = 1/x`),
443	the target coordinate `1/(λx) = u/λ` is regular because `λ ∈ 𝔾_m` is invertible. The two
444	chart-restrictions agree on `(𝔸¹ ∖ {0}) × 𝔾_m`, so they glue via
445	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms`.
446	
447	The companion lemma `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` exposes the load-bearing fixed-point
448	property `σ_×(0, λ) = 0` for all `λ ∈ 𝔾_m`, packaged as the `W`-axis-collapse hypothesis
449	that `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) consumes. -/
450	
451	/-- **The `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`** in `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.
452	
453	Scaffold body — iter-165 lands the *type signature* (the concrete object the iter-166
454	proof refactor of `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` consumes); the chartwise glue body
455	is iter-166's lane (or an even later sub-build if `Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms` requires
456	further infrastructure). -/
457	def gmScalingP1 (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
458	    ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⊗ Gm kbar ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
459	  sorry
460	
461	/-- **The load-bearing fixed-point property of `σ_×`:** at the scaling fixed point
462	`0 ∈ ℙ¹`, the morphism `σ_×(0, ·) : 𝔾_m → ℙ¹` is the constant morphism at `0`. That is,
463	the composite `(0 ≫ toUnit) × 𝟙 : 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹` equals `toUnit ≫ 0`.
464	
465	This is precisely the `W`-axis-collapse hypothesis `_hf` that
466	`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) consumes when applied with `V = ℙ¹` proper,
467	`W = 𝔾_m`, base points `0 ∈ ℙ¹`, `1 ∈ 𝔾_m`.
468	
469	Scaffold body — iter-165 lands the *statement* (matching the rigidity consumer's `_hf`
470	shape); the proof body is iter-166's lane (it reduces to a chart-level computation: on
471	`𝔸¹ × 𝔾_m`, `(0, λ) ↦ λ·0 = 0` is a defequal ring-map check). -/
472	lemma gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
473	    lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar)) ≫
474	        gmScalingP1 kbar =
475	      toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar := by
476	  sorry
477	
478	/-! ### (E) Product-stability instances on `ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m`
479	
480	These four instances are exported for Lane B's consumer `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux`
481	(in `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`), so its previously local `haveI ... := by sorry`
482	ad-hoc scaffolds collapse to `inferInstance`. Each instance is justified as follows:
483	
484	* `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).hom` is locally of finite type — by composition with `pullback.fst`
485	  (`LocallyOfFiniteType` is `IsStableUnderComposition` and `IsStableUnderBaseChange`,
486	  with both factors LOFT).
487	* `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).hom` is geometrically irreducible — scaffold (Mathlib gap: `GeometricallyIrreducible`
488	  on `Gm.hom` needs the alg-closed-base reduction, currently not bridged).
489	* `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left` is reduced — scaffold (Mathlib gap: `Smooth → GeometricallyReduced`
490	  not shipped at scheme level).
491	* `ℙ¹` is reduced — scaffold (Mathlib gap: `IsReduced (Proj 𝒜)` for a domain graded ring
492	  is not shipped; would close via `IsReduced.of_openCover` over `Proj.affineOpenCover` once
493	  `HomogeneousLocalization.Away`'s domain property is bridged).
494	* `Gm.hom` is geometrically irreducible — scaffold (Mathlib gap: see above). -/
495	
496	/-- **`(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).hom` is locally of finite type.** Decomposes as
497	`pullback.fst ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom`; `LocallyOfFiniteType` is stable under composition
498	and pullback (Mathlib's `locallyOfFiniteType_comp`,
499	`locallyOfFiniteType_isStableUnderBaseChange`). -/
500	instance projGm_locallyOfFiniteType (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
501	    LocallyOfFiniteType ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom := by
502	  change LocallyOfFiniteType
503	    (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom)
504	  infer_instance
505	
506	/-- **`ℙ¹` is reduced.** Project-side scaffold sorry (Mathlib does not ship `IsReduced (Proj 𝒜)`
507	for a domain graded ring; would close via `IsReduced.of_openCover` over `Proj.affineOpenCover`
508	once `HomogeneousLocalization.Away` is bridged to `IsDomain` for the standard ℕ-grading
509	on `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`). Exported here for Lane B (replaces its inline `haveI hP1red`).
510	
511	The strategy:
512	1. `IsReduced.of_openCover` over `Proj.affineOpenCover (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)`.
513	2. Each chart `Spec (HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 (X_i))` reduces to showing the ring is a
514	   domain.
515	3. `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 X_i` for the standard ℕ-grading on `k̄[X_0, X_1]` is
516	   isomorphic to `k̄[X_{1-i}/X_i]` (polynomial ring in one variable over a field, a domain). -/
517	instance projectiveLineBar_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
518	    IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left := by
519	  -- Strategy: `IsReduced.of_openCover` over `Proj.affineOpenCover`, each chart being
520	  -- `Spec (HomogeneousLocalization.Away …)`. Each chart is a domain ⟹ reduced, but the
521	  -- `HomogeneousLocalization.Away`-is-domain bridge is currently a Mathlib gap.
522	  sorry
523	
524	/-- **`𝔾_m` is geometrically irreducible over `Spec k̄`.** Scaffold (Mathlib gap: the
525	direct `GeometricallyIrreducible` consequence of `IrreducibleSpace + Spec(domain over alg
526	closed)` is not bridged; the analogist's recipe would require base-change reduction via
527	`IsAlgClosed`-fixed bridges that are absent at scheme level).
528	
529	Exported here for Lane B and for the `projGm_geomIrred` derivation. -/
530	instance gm_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
531	    GeometricallyIrreducible (Gm kbar).hom := by
532	  sorry
533	
534	/-- **`(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).hom` is geometrically irreducible.** Derives from the individual factors
535	via `GeometricallyIrreducible.comp` (with `UniversallyOpen` discharged for free by smoothness
536	of each factor). The `(X ⊗ Y).hom = pullback.fst ≫ X.hom` defeq unfolds, then
537	`GeometricallyIrreducible.comp` chains `pullback.fst`'s GI (by base-change stability of GI
538	from `gm_geomIrred`) with `projectiveLineBar_geomIrred`.
539	
540	Exported here for Lane B (replaces its inline `haveI hProdGI`). Axiom-clean given the
541	individual GI scaffolds. -/
542	instance projGm_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
543	    GeometricallyIrreducible ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom := by
544	  change GeometricallyIrreducible
545	    (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom)
546	  exact GeometricallyIrreducible.comp _ _
547	
548	/-- **`(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left` is reduced.** Project-side scaffold sorry (Mathlib gap: the
549	`Smooth → GeometricallyReduced` bridge is missing at scheme level, so the standard
550	`isReduced_of_flat_of_isLocallyNoetherian` route is not directly applicable without
551	adding scheme-level infrastructure beyond an iter-167 lane's scope).
552	
553	The chart-local alternative: cover `ProjectiveLineBar ⊗ Gm` by `Spec(k̄[t, λ, λ⁻¹])`
554	(a domain over k̄) using the product of `Proj.affineOpenCover` and the affine
555	`Gm = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]`. Each chart is a domain ⟹ reduced. Both rely on bridges currently
556	absent in Mathlib (`HomogeneousLocalization.Away`-is-domain plus
557	`tensor-of-domains-over-field-is-domain`).
558	
559	Exported here for Lane B (replaces its inline `haveI hProdRed`). -/
560	instance projGm_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
561	    IsReduced ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).left := by
562	  -- Strategy: chart-local IsReduced via Proj.affineOpenCover product, each chart a domain.
563	  -- Currently sorry: blocked by Mathlib gap on Smooth → GeometricallyReduced.
564	  sorry
565	
566	end AlgebraicGeometry

8	/-!
9	# The Abel-Jacobi map
10	
11	The Abel-Jacobi map from a smooth, proper curve to its Jacobian, and the universal property
12	of the Jacobian as the Albanese variety.
13	
14	## Status (iteration 073 — Phase E closes by reduction)
15	
16	After the iter-073 refactor of `Jacobian C := (jacobianWitness C).J` (no genus-0 dite),
17	all three protected declarations reduce uniformly to the Albanese universal property
18	carried by `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P : IsAlbanese C P (jacobianWitness C).J`:
19	
20	* `ofCurve P := ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).ofCurve`.
21	* `comp_ofCurve P := ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).comp_ofCurve`.
22	* `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp P f hf := ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).exists_unique_ofCurve_comp f hf`.
23	
24	The genus-0 rigidity content previously sitting at the existence step of
25	`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` (classical rigidity `Hom(ℙ¹, A) = A(k)`) is now absorbed
26	into the single deferred existence claim `nonempty_jacobianWitness` in
27	`Jacobian.lean`: the witness's `isAlbaneseFor P` field must verify the Albanese property
28	for genus-0 curves too, which is precisely the rigidity content.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
38	
39	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
40	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
41	  [IsProper C.hom]
42	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
43	
44	namespace Jacobian
45	
46	variable {C} in
47	-- data
48	/-- The Abel-Jacobi map from a smooth, proper curve to its Jacobian associated
49	to a `k`-rational point of `C`. Projects the Albanese universal morphism carried
50	by the witness `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`. -/
51	noncomputable def ofCurve (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C) : C ⟶ Jacobian C := by
52	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
53	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
54	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
55	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
56	  exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).ofCurve
57	
58	variable {C} in
59	/-- The Abel-Jacobi map sends the `k`-rational point `P` to `0`, where `0` (denoted by `η` below) is
60	the neutral element of the group scheme `Jacobian C`. Projects the pointed-property
61	of the Albanese witness `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`. -/
62	lemma comp_ofCurve (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C) :
63	    P ≫ ofCurve P = η[Jacobian C] := by
64	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
65	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
66	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
67	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
68	  exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).comp_ofCurve
69	
70	variable {C} in
71	/--
72	The universal property of the Jacobian variety: For any abelian variety `A`,
73	any morphism `f : C ⟶ A` such that `f(P) = 0` factors uniquely through the
74	Jacobian of `C`.
75	In other words, `Jacobian C` is the Albanese variety of `C`.
76	
77	The existence-and-uniqueness data is projected unconditionally from the witness
78	`(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`. The genus-0 rigidity content (`Hom(ℙ¹, A) = A(k)`,
79	Mumford §4) is absorbed into the deferred existence claim `nonempty_jacobianWitness`,
80	since the witness must verify the Albanese property for genus-0 curves too.
81	-/
82	theorem exists_unique_ofCurve_comp (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C)
83	    {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [Smooth A.hom] [IsProper A.hom] [GrpObj A]
84	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
85	    ∃! (g : Jacobian C ⟶ A), f = ofCurve P ≫ g := by
86	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
87	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
88	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
89	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
90	  exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).exists_unique_ofCurve_comp f hf
91	
92	end Jacobian
93	
94	end AlgebraicGeometry
95

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK
8	
9	/-!
10	# Genus of a smooth proper curve
11	
12	The genus of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field.
13	
14	## Status (closed iteration 011 — `genus`)
15	
16	The iter-011 refactor added the `noncomputable` modifier (user-authorised
17	`2026-05-07`) and an import of `AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK`
18	so the body uses the project's `ModuleCat k`-flavoured cohomology
19	`Scheme.HModule` (iter-009) of the structure sheaf
20	`Scheme.toModuleKSheaf` (iter-006). The body is the probe-confirmed
21	one-liner
22	
23	    Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)
24	
25	which is the honest mathematical definition `dim_k H^1(C, O_C)`.
26	
27	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Genus.tex` for the informal proof sketch and
28	`.archon/STRATEGY.md` for the multi-phase build-out plan.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
38	
39	/-- The genus of a smooth proper curve. -/
40	noncomputable def genus {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
41	    [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
42	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : ℕ :=
43	  Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)
44	
45	end AlgebraicGeometry
46

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus
7	
8	/-! # The Jacobian of a smooth proper curve
9	
10	The Jacobian of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field, equipped with
11	its structure as an abelian variety.
12	
13	## Status (Phase C scaffolding)
14	
15	The Jacobian is defined uniformly as the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness
16	(`JacobianWitness`) for the curve `C`; the four protected instances on `Jacobian C` all
17	project from the witness directly.
18	
19	This file currently contains TWO `sorry`-bodied declarations (the Phase-C scaffolding
20	inventory):
21	
22	1. `genusZeroWitness` (iter-127 scaffold): a `JacobianWitness C` for a genus-`0` curve,
23	   with underlying scheme `Spec k`. The body is a `sorry` to be closed once pieces
24	   (i)+(ii)+(iii) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile land (iter-138+); the
25	   substantive mathematical content is the genus-`0` rigidity statement
26	   `rigidity_over_kbar` (M2.a; `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar`).
27	2. `nonempty_jacobianWitness` (Phase-C OFF-LIMITS sorry): existence of an Albanese
28	   witness for an arbitrary smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve; gated on
29	   M2 + M3 closure (Albanese existence in genus `≥ 1` plus genus-`0` rigidity).
30	
31	The file contains:
32	- `IsAlbanese`: the Albanese universal property for a pointed curve.
33	- `IsAlbanese.unique`: uniqueness of the Albanese object up to canonical isomorphism.
34	- `JacobianWitness`: a bundled candidate Albanese object together with the universal
35	  property uniformly over the choice of $k$-rational marked point.
36	- `genusZeroWitness`: an Albanese witness for a genus-`0` curve, body `sorry`
37	  (iter-127 scaffold; closure deferred to iter-138+).
38	- `nonempty_jacobianWitness`: existence of an Albanese witness for every curve —
39	  the headline Phase-C sorry, absorbing both higher-genus Albanese existence and
40	  genus-`0` rigidity.
41	- `Jacobian` and its four protected instances, each obtained by projection from the
42	  Albanese witness.
43	
44	### Forbidden shortcut (sanity check)
45	
46	Defining `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))` *unconditionally* (the terminal object,
47	i.e. `Spec k`) compiles and discharges three of the four instances for free, but the
48	fourth instance `SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (𝟙_ _).hom` would force `genus C = 0`,
49	which is mathematically wrong for any curve of genus `≥ 1`. The terminal-object definition
50	is therefore forbidden when `genus C > 0`. The witness-based definition avoids this
51	issue: in genus `0` the witness's underlying scheme `J` is `Spec k`, and in genus `> 0`
52	the witness's `J` is the honest Albanese object.
53	-/
54	
55	set_option autoImplicit false
56	
57	universe u
58	
59	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
60	
61	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
62	
63	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
64	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
65	  [IsProper C.hom]
66	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
67	
68	/-- The Albanese universal property for a pointed smooth proper curve `(C, P)`.
69	An object `J` (with abelian-variety structure) is the Albanese of `(C, P)` if it
70	receives a universal pointed morphism `C ⟶ J` sending `P` to the identity. -/
71	def IsAlbanese (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C)
72	    (J : Over (Spec (.of k)))
73	    [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] : Prop :=
74	  ∃ α : C ⟶ J, P ≫ α = η[J] ∧
75	    ∀ {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom]
76	      [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (_ : P ≫ f = η[A]),
77	      ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = α ≫ g
78	
79	namespace IsAlbanese
80	
81	noncomputable def ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
82	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
83	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) : C ⟶ J :=
84	  Classical.choose h
85	
86	lemma comp_ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
87	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
88	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) :
89	    P ≫ h.ofCurve = η[J] :=
90	  (Classical.choose_spec h).1
91	
92	lemma exists_unique_ofCurve_comp {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
94	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J)
95	    {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
96	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
97	    ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = h.ofCurve ≫ g :=
98	  (Classical.choose_spec h).2 f hf
99	
100	/-- The Albanese object is unique up to a unique isomorphism compatible with the
101	universal morphisms. -/
102	theorem unique {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))} {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C}
103	    {J₁ J₂ : Over (Spec (.of k))}
104	    [GrpObj J₁] [IsProper J₁.hom] [Smooth J₁.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₁.hom]
105	    [GrpObj J₂] [IsProper J₂.hom] [Smooth J₂.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₂.hom]
106	    (h₁ : IsAlbanese C P J₁) (h₂ : IsAlbanese C P J₂) :
107	    ∃! (e : J₁ ⟶ J₂), h₂.ofCurve = h₁.ofCurve ≫ e := by
108	  have ⟨g, hg_eq, hg_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
109	  have ⟨h, hh_eq, hh_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
110	  have ⟨g₁, hg₁_eq, hg₁_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
111	  have g₁_eq_id : g₁ = 𝟙 J₁ := by
112	    refine (hg₁_unique (𝟙 J₁) ?_).symm
113	    simp
114	  have ggh_eq_g₁ : g ≫ h = g₁ := by
115	    refine hg₁_unique (g ≫ h) ?_
116	    change h₁.ofCurve = h₁.ofCurve ≫ (g ≫ h)
117	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hg_eq, ← hh_eq]
118	  have hgh : g ≫ h = 𝟙 J₁ := by rw [ggh_eq_g₁, g₁_eq_id]
119	  have ⟨k₂, hk₂_eq, hk₂_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
120	  have k₂_eq_id : k₂ = 𝟙 J₂ := by
121	    refine (hk₂_unique (𝟙 J₂) ?_).symm
122	    simp
123	  have hhg_eq_k₂ : h ≫ g = k₂ := by
124	    refine hk₂_unique (h ≫ g) ?_
125	    change h₂.ofCurve = h₂.ofCurve ≫ (h ≫ g)
126	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hh_eq, ← hg_eq]
127	  have hhg : h ≫ g = 𝟙 J₂ := by rw [hhg_eq_k₂, k₂_eq_id]
128	  exact ⟨g, hg_eq, fun g' hg' => hg_unique g' hg'⟩
129	
130	end IsAlbanese
131	
132	/-- The identity morphism on `Spec k` is geometrically irreducible.
133	This is a small helper needed for the genus-0 case of `Jacobian`. -/
134	lemma geometricallyIrreducible_id_Spec (k : Type u) [Field k] :
135	    GeometricallyIrreducible (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))) where
136	  geometrically_irreducibleSpace := by
137	    intro K _ y Z fst snd h
138	    haveI : IsIso snd := h.isIso_snd_of_isIso
139	    exact ObjectProperty.prop_of_iso (P := (IrreducibleSpace · : ObjectProperty Scheme))
140	      (asIso snd).symm inferInstance
141	
142	/-- A bundled Albanese witness for the smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve
143	`C`: a candidate group scheme `J` (with proper, smooth, geometrically irreducible
144	structure, smooth of relative dimension `genus C`), and a proof that `J` satisfies
145	the Albanese universal property of Definition `IsAlbanese` for `C` *for every choice
146	of $k$-rational marked point* `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`.
147	
148	Mathematically, the underlying scheme `J` (and its group/proper/smooth/irreducible
149	structure) is intrinsic to `C`; only the universal morphism `C ⟶ J` depends on the
150	choice of marked point `P`. Encoding `isAlbaneseFor` as a `∀ P, IsAlbanese …` field
151	keeps the bundle independent of any specific pointing, which is what
152	`AbelJacobi.Jacobian.ofCurve P` needs in order to project the Albanese morphism for
153	an *arbitrary* input `P`.
154	
155	The smoothness witness is recorded at the specific relative dimension `genus C`,
156	which is the form required by `Jacobian.smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`. -/
157	structure JacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
158	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] where
159	  /-- The candidate Albanese object. -/
160	  J : Over (Spec (.of k))
161	  /-- The group-scheme structure on `J`. -/
162	  grpObj : GrpObj J
163	  /-- `J` is proper over `k`. -/
164	  proper : IsProper J.hom
165	  /-- `J` is smooth over `k`. -/
166	  smooth : Smooth J.hom
167	  /-- `J` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
168	  geomIrred : GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom
169	  /-- `J` is smooth of relative dimension `genus C` (so dim `J = genus C`). -/
170	  smoothGenus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) J.hom
171	  /-- The Albanese universal-property data: for every choice of $k$-rational marked
172	  point `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`, the scheme `J` together with its group/proper/smooth/irreducible
173	  structure satisfies the Albanese universal property for the pointed curve `(C, P)`. -/
174	  isAlbaneseFor : ∀ (P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C), @IsAlbanese k _ C P J grpObj proper smooth geomIrred
175	
176	/-- The Albanese witness for a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C`
177	of genus `0` over `k`. The underlying scheme is `Spec k` (the terminal object), with
178	the trivial group structure, smoothness of relative dimension `0` (matching the
179	hypothesis `genus C = 0`), properness, and geometric irreducibility. The
180	`isAlbaneseFor` field encodes the universal property: for every $k$-rational
181	marked point `P` of `C`, the universal morphism `C ⟶ Spec k` is the unique
182	terminal map `toUnit C`; the substantive content is the rigidity statement
183	`rigidity_over_kbar` (M2.a; `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar`), gated on the
184	shared cotangent-vanishing pile (iter-129+).
185	
186	**Status**: iter-127 scaffold — body is `sorry`. The body closure is iter-138+
187	work, after pieces (i)+(ii)+(iii) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile land.
188	
189	**Iter-135**: now consumed by the genus-stratified body of
190	`nonempty_jacobianWitness` (Change 2.1 of iter-135 refactor); body
191	closure remains iter-138+ work but the witness is now load-bearing
192	for the protected `nonempty_jacobianWitness` chain.
193	
194	**Iter-155 (skeleton)**: the bare `sorry` is replaced by the blueprint's
195	explicit terminal-object witness (Jacobian.tex § C.3). The underlying
196	scheme is `J := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec k))` (whose `.hom` is defeq `𝟙 (Spec k)`),
197	and all six structural fields close from Mathlib instances on the identity
198	plus the project helper `geometricallyIrreducible_id_Spec`. The single
199	remaining mathematical gap is localized in the `isAlbaneseFor` field, and
200	within it to exactly the two obstructions the blueprint identifies:
201	  * `key : f = toUnit C ≫ η[A]` — the genus-0 RIGIDITY content (C.2). This
202	    is `rigidity_over_kbar` (over `k̄`, NAMED GAP) base-changed and then
203	    *descended* to the base field `k` along the faithfully-flat surjection
204	    `Spec k̄ → Spec k` (C.2.f, via `Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective`). NOTE:
205	    `rigidity_over_kbar` additionally requires `[CharZero kbar]`, so the
206	    char-`p` arm of this `[Field k]` goal is *also* unbacked.
207	  * the uniqueness clause — cancels the epimorphism `toUnit C` (the structure
208	    morphism `C.hom` is faithfully flat + surjective, hence epi). -/
209	noncomputable def genusZeroWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
210	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
211	    (h : genus C = 0) :
212	    JacobianWitness C where
213	  J := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))
214	  grpObj := inferInstance
215	  -- `(𝟙_).hom` is defeq `𝟙 (Spec k)`, but instance search does not unfold it;
216	  -- supply each instance for the identity and let term-elaboration close by defeq.
217	  proper := (inferInstance : IsProper (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))))
218	  smooth := (inferInstance : Smooth (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))))
219	  geomIrred := geometricallyIrreducible_id_Spec k
220	  smoothGenus := by
221	    rw [h]; exact (inferInstance : SmoothOfRelativeDimension 0 (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))))
222	  isAlbaneseFor := by
223	    -- For every `k`-rational marked point `P`, exhibit the Albanese datum for
224	    -- the pointed curve `(C, P)` with `J = 𝟙_` (blueprint C.2 / C.3).
225	    intro P
226	    -- The universal pointed morphism is the unique terminal map `toUnit C`.
227	    refine ⟨toUnit C, ?_, ?_⟩
228	    · -- Pointed condition: both `P ≫ toUnit C` and `η[𝟙_]` are maps `𝟙_ ⟶ 𝟙_`,
229	      -- equal by uniqueness of morphisms into the terminal object.
230	      exact toUnit_unique _ _
231	    · -- Universal factorisation: given `f : C ⟶ A` with `P ≫ f = η[A]`, the
232	      -- factoring morphism `𝟙_ ⟶ A` is the identity element `η[A]`.
233	      intro A _ _ _ _ f _hf
234	      -- The genus-0 RIGIDITY keystone (C.2): `f` equals the constant morphism
235	      -- at the identity. Over `k̄` this is `rigidity_over_kbar`; over the base
236	      -- field `k` it is obtained by base-change + faithfully-flat descent
237	      -- (C.2.f). NAMED GAP. Iter-156 verified the precise blockers that keep
238	      -- this UN-dispatchable from within `Jacobian.lean` — three independent
239	      -- gates, all out-of-file / plan-level:
240	      --   (1) IMPORT CYCLE. `rigidity_over_kbar` lives in
241	      --       `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar`, which imports `Rigidity`, which
242	      --       imports `Jacobian`. So `Jacobian.lean` CANNOT import it (would cycle
243	      --       `Jacobian → RigidityKbar → Rigidity → Jacobian`); `rigidity_over_kbar`
244	      --       is literally "unknown identifier" here. The rigidity stack must be
245	      --       relocated UPSTREAM of `Jacobian` (or genus-0 rigidity restated in an
246	      --       importable location) before any prover can wire this — a plan-level
247	      --       refactor, not a prover edit.
248	      --   (2) CHAR-`p` LOGICAL GAP. `rigidity_over_kbar` assumes `[CharZero kbar]`;
249	      --       `CharZero (AlgebraicClosure k)` is UNSYNTHESIZABLE from `[Field k]`
250	      --       (false in char `p`). Verified iter-156: `inferInstance` fails. Even
251	      --       with the import + a closed body, the char-`p` arm of this `[Field k]`
252	      --       goal stays unbacked until route (c) (theorem-of-the-cube → rigidity
253	      --       lemma, Milne §I; Jacobian.tex §sec:av_rigidity_route_c) DROPS the
254	      --       `[CharZero]` hypothesis from `rigidity_over_kbar`.
255	      --   (3) BASE-CHANGE FUNCTOR MISSING. The descent C.2.f needs a base-change
256	      --       functor `Over (Spec k) → Over (Spec k̄)` transferring all 7
257	      --       instances + genus stability, then the epi-cancellation along the
258	      --       faithfully-flat surjection (`Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective` — that
259	      --       last step alone IS in Mathlib). No such functor exists in the project
260	      --       or is assembled in Mathlib (`b80f227`); it is a multi-iter sub-build.
261	      -- Until (1)+(2)+(3) clear, no honest in-file term closes this; a speculative
262	      -- base-change scaffold would be unconsumable (cycle + char-`p`) and would only
263	      -- inflate the sorry count, so the gate is recorded rather than papered over.
264	      have key : f = toUnit C ≫ η[A] := by
265	        sorry
266	      refine ⟨η[A], key, ?_⟩
267	      -- Uniqueness: any `g'` with `f = toUnit C ≫ g'` equals `η[A]`, by cancelling
268	      -- the epimorphism `toUnit C`. Its underlying scheme morphism is `C.hom`
269	      -- (`Over.toUnit_left`), which is faithfully flat (smooth ⟹ flat) and
270	      -- surjective (a geometrically irreducible curve over a field is nonempty and
271	      -- `Spec k` is a single point), hence an epimorphism (`Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective`).
272	      intro g' hg'
273	      have hcancel : toUnit C ≫ g' = toUnit C ≫ η[A] := by rw [← hg', ← key]
274	      have hepiL : Epi (toUnit C).left := by
275	        rw [Over.toUnit_left]; exact Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective C.hom
276	      haveI : Epi (toUnit C) := Over.epi_of_epi_left _
277	      exact (cancel_epi (toUnit C)).mp hcancel
278	
279	/-- The Albanese witness for a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C`
280	of positive genus `g ≥ 1` over `k`. The underlying scheme is the Albanese
281	variety of `C` — classically the connected component of the identity of
282	`Pic_{C/k}`, or equivalently the Stein factorisation of the Abel–Jacobi
283	morphism on `Sym^g(C)`. Both constructions require infrastructure not yet
284	in Mathlib (FGA representability of the Picard functor for Route A;
285	symmetric powers + finite-group quotients + Stein factorisation +
286	Brill–Noether–Riemann–Roch for Route B); the iter-123 M3 route audit
287	(`analogies/m3-route-audit.md`) records both at midpoint ~6500 and
288	~9000 LOC respectively.
289	
290	**Status**: iter-134 scaffold — body is `sorry`. The body closure is M3
291	work, currently OFF-CRITICAL-PATH until M2 closes (per STRATEGY.md
292	§ M3, user-escalation-pending).
293	
294	**Iter-135**: the genus-stratified body restructure of
295	`nonempty_jacobianWitness` is now in place (Change 2.1 of iter-135
296	refactor); this scaffold is the positive-genus arm. Body closure
297	remains M3 work (off-critical-path; user-escalation-pending per
298	`analogies/m3-route-audit.md`). -/
299	noncomputable def positiveGenusWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
300	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
301	    (hg : 0 < genus C) :
302	    JacobianWitness C :=
303	  sorry
304	
305	/-- Existence of an Albanese witness for every smooth proper geometrically irreducible
306	curve. This is one of the two open mathematical sorries of the Phase-C Jacobian
307	scaffolding (the other being `genusZeroWitness`); both are scheduled for body
308	closure post M2 + M3 per STRATEGY.md. It packages all five protected sorries
309	(`Jacobian`, `instGrpObj`, `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`, `instIsProper`,
310	`instGeometricallyIrreducible`) into a single existence hypothesis, *and* records
311	that the Albanese property of the underlying scheme `J` is uniform over every
312	choice of $k$-rational marked point `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C` (via the `isAlbaneseFor`
313	field of `JacobianWitness`).
314	
315	Mathematically this corresponds to the construction of the Albanese variety of `C`,
316	classically via symmetric powers $\mathrm{Sym}^g(C)$ and the Abel--Jacobi map
317	(Brill--Noether--Riemann--Roch), or equivalently as the identity component of the
318	Picard scheme. Both routes require infrastructure not yet available in Mathlib
319	(quotients of schemes by finite group actions; FGA representability), so the
320	existence is assumed.
321	
322	**Iter-135 body restructure**: the body is now a `by_cases h :
323	genus C = 0` decomposition consuming `genusZeroWitness` (genus-0 arm)
324	and `positiveGenusWitness` (positive-genus arm). Both arms' bodies
325	remain `sorry` (M2.b body closure iter-138+; M3 body closure
326	off-critical-path); the restructure converts the inline `sorry` here
327	into honest delegation to the two scaffolds, removing one inline-`sorry`
328	site without prejudging either arm's body closure. -/
329	theorem nonempty_jacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
330	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
331	    Nonempty (JacobianWitness C) := by
332	  by_cases h : genus C = 0
333	  · exact ⟨genusZeroWitness C h⟩
334	  · exact ⟨positiveGenusWitness C (Nat.pos_of_ne_zero h)⟩
335	
336	/-- A choice of Albanese witness for `C`, extracted via `Classical.choice`.
337	Used to define `Jacobian C` and to discharge each of the four protected instances
338	on it. -/
339	noncomputable def jacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
340	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
341	    JacobianWitness C :=
342	  Classical.choice (nonempty_jacobianWitness C)
343	
344	-- data
345	/-- The Jacobian of a smooth, proper curve over a field `k`.
346	
347	The Jacobian is the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness for `C` (see
348	`JacobianWitness` and `nonempty_jacobianWitness`); the existence of such a
349	witness is one of the two open mathematical sorries of the Phase-C scaffolding
350	(the other being `genusZeroWitness`), both scheduled for body closure post
351	M2 + M3 per STRATEGY.md. The genus-`0` specialisation is implicit in the
352	witness — a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme over `k` of
353	relative dimension `0` is `Spec k` — and the genus-`0` rigidity content is
354	absorbed into `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. -/
355	noncomputable def Jacobian (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
356	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
357	  (jacobianWitness C).J
358	
359	namespace Jacobian
360	
361	/-! ## The Jacobian of `C` is an abelian variety. -/
362	
363	-- data
364	/-- The group scheme structure on the Jacobian of the curve `C`. -/
365	noncomputable instance instGrpObj : GrpObj (Jacobian C) := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
366	
367	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is smooth of relative dimension `g` over `k`, where `g` is the
368	genus of `C`. -/
369	instance smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (Jacobian C).hom :=
370	  (jacobianWitness C).smoothGenus
371	
372	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is proper over `k`. -/
373	instance instIsProper : IsProper (Jacobian C).hom := (jacobianWitness C).proper
374	
375	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
376	instance instGeometricallyIrreducible : GeometricallyIrreducible (Jacobian C).hom :=
377	  (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
378	
379	end Jacobian
380	
381	end AlgebraicGeometry
382

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian
7	
8	/-!
9	# Rigidity for morphisms of schemes (scheme-level form)
10	
11	A self-contained Phase E helper: two morphisms `g₁, g₂ : X ⟶ Y` between
12	schemes over `Spec k` whose restrictions to a non-empty open `U ⊆ X` agree
13	as scheme morphisms — for `X` reduced and geometrically irreducible over
14	`Spec k` and `Y` separated over `Spec k` — agree everywhere. Independent of
15	`AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian C` and provable from current Mathlib alone
16	(Stacks 0BFD plus standard separatedness/irreducibility lemmas).
17	
18	The historical motivation is Mumford's §4 rigidity for morphisms of group
19	schemes; the formalized statement is the minimal-hypothesis scheme-level
20	form, which the Mumford group-scheme rigidity follows from by specialization.
21	
22	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Rigidity.tex`.
23	
24	## Status
25	
26	`Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen` — rigidity of morphisms agreeing as scheme
27	maps on a non-empty open of a reduced (geometrically) irreducible source
28	with separated target — is closed. See the "Hypothesis history" block below
29	for the discussion of why the original point-wise hypothesis was
30	strengthened to scheme-level equality on `U`.
31	-/
32	
33	set_option autoImplicit false
34	
35	universe u
36	
37	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
38	
39	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
40	
41	variable {k : Type u} [Field k]
42	
43	/-!
44	## Hypothesis history
45	
46	### Scheme-level hypothesis (iter 003)
47	
48	The original scaffold from iter 002 had hypothesis
49	`h : ∀ x ∈ U, g₁.left.base x = g₂.left.base x`
50	(point-wise topological equality on `U`). This is too weak: in characteristic
51	`p`, the absolute Frobenius `F : X → X` of an elliptic curve over `𝔽_p` agrees
52	topologically with the identity on all of `X` (the identity on the underlying
53	topological space) but `F ≠ id` as scheme morphisms — they differ on stalks
54	(`a ↦ a` vs `a ↦ a^p`). Hence the symmetric-form rigidity statement
55	`g₁.base = g₂.base on U ⇒ g₁ = g₂` is mathematically false.
56	
57	We strengthen the hypothesis to scheme-level equality on `U`:
58	`h : U.ι ≫ g₁.left = U.ι ≫ g₂.left`. This is the version that is actually
59	needed at the use site (uniqueness half of `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`):
60	the application produces *scheme-level* agreement on a non-empty open
61	(through the symmetric-power surjection), not merely topological agreement.
62	
63	We also add `[IsReduced X.left]` because Mathlib `b80f227` lacks the lemma
64	`Smooth ⇒ IsReduced` for schemes over a field (the closest is the perfect-base
65	case `Scheme.Hom.dense_smoothLocus_of_perfectField`). This is a small Mathlib
66	gap that the downstream consumer must satisfy via the smoothness + irreducibility
67	of the curve / Jacobian (e.g. via `IsIntegral`).
68	
69	### Unused-hypothesis cleanup (iter 125)
70	
71	Eight decorative hypotheses (`{n m : ℕ}`, `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n X.hom]`,
72	`[IsProper X.hom]`, `[GrpObj X]`, `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension m Y.hom]`,
73	`[GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom]`, `[GrpObj Y]`, and the strengthening of
74	`[IsSeparated Y.hom]` to `[IsProper Y.hom]`) were dropped per
75	`analogies/rigidity-refactor.md`. The declaration was also renamed from
76	`GrpObj.eq_of_eqOnOpen` to `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen`, mirroring
77	Mathlib's `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'` naming idiom
78	(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Separated:319–322`).
79	-/
80	
81	/-- Rigidity for morphisms of schemes (scheme-level form): two morphisms
82	`g₁, g₂ : X ⟶ Y` between schemes over `Spec k` whose restrictions to a
83	non-empty open `U ⊆ X` agree as scheme morphisms — for `X` reduced and
84	geometrically irreducible over `Spec k` and `Y` separated over `Spec k` —
85	agree everywhere.
86	
87	This is the `Over (Spec (.of k))`-bundled specialization of Mathlib's
88	`ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'`. The Mumford group-scheme rigidity
89	statement (a morphism vanishing on a non-empty open is zero) is
90	interderivable with this form using the group law on the target. -/
91	theorem Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen
92	    {X Y : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    [IsSeparated Y.hom]
94	    [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
95	    [IsReduced X.left]
96	    (g₁ g₂ : X ⟶ Y) (U : X.left.Opens) (hU : (U : Set X.left).Nonempty)
97	    (h : (U.ι : (U : X.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ X.left) ≫ g₁.left =
98	      (U.ι : (U : X.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ X.left) ≫ g₂.left) :
99	    g₁ = g₂ := by
100	  -- (1) Provide the separatedness instance under the `OverClass.fromOver`
101	  --     form `Y.left ↘ Spec (.of k)` so that the downstream typeclass
102	  --     search at `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'` finds it.
103	  haveI : IsSeparated (Y.left ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)) :=
104	    (‹IsSeparated Y.hom› : IsSeparated Y.hom)
105	  -- (2) `X.left` is irreducible: `Spec k` is a single point (`Unique`,
106	  --     hence `Subsingleton + Nonempty`), and `GeometricallyIrreducible`
107	  --     over a subsingleton base implies `IrreducibleSpace`.
108	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace X.left :=
109	    GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton X.hom
110	  -- (3) `U.ι : U.toScheme ⟶ X.left` is dominant: a non-empty open in an
111	  --     irreducible space is dense, and a dense open immersion is dominant.
112	  haveI : IsDominant (U.ι : (U : X.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ X.left) :=
113	    Scheme.PartialMap.Opens.isDominant_ι (IsOpen.dense U.isOpen hU)
114	  -- (4) Promote `g₁ = g₂` from the underlying scheme equality.
115	  --     The `Scheme.Over` and `IsOver` instances for `X.left`, `Y.left`,
116	  --     `g₁.left`, `g₂.left` over `Spec (.of k)` are inferred globally
117	  --     from the `Over (Spec (.of k))` category structure
118	  --     (`OverClass.fromOver`).
119	  refine Over.OverMorphism.ext ?_
120	  exact ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated' (S := Spec (.of k))
121	    (X := X.left) (Y := Y.left) (f := g₁.left) (g := g₂.left) U.ι h
122	
123	end AlgebraicGeometry
124

4	Authors: Christian Merten

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Rigidity
7	
8	/-!
9	# Rigidity over `k̄`: morphisms `ℙ¹_{k̄} → A_{k̄}` are constant
10	
11	The keystone classical input for the M2.a sub-step of the genus-0
12	Albanese-witness argument (Jacobian.tex § C.2): morphisms from the
13	projective line `ℙ¹_{k̄}` to a smooth proper geometrically irreducible
14	group scheme `A_{k̄}` over an algebraically closed field `k̄` that hit
15	the identity at a `k̄`-rational point are the constant morphism at the
16	identity. Mumford, *Abelian Varieties*, Chapter~II~§4.
17	
18	See `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex`.
19	
20	## Status (iter-126 scaffold)
21	
22	The named declaration `rigidity_over_kbar` is scaffolded with a single
23	`sorry` body. The body closure is gated on the shared cotangent-
24	vanishing Mathlib pile (`analogies/cotangent-vanishing-pile.md`,
25	iter-129+) per STRATEGY.md § M2.a + § M2.d-alt. The proof decomposition
26	into sub-steps C.2.b (reduction to `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen`),
27	C.2.c (image-dimension dichotomy), C.2.d (the cotangent-vanishing
28	keystone), and C.2.e (set-to-scheme promotion) is documented in
29	`RigidityKbar.tex` § "Proof decomposition".
30	
31	## Encoding choice (iter-126 refactor agent note)
32	
33	The directive offered two encodings of the source `ℙ¹_{k̄}`: a literal
34	`Over.mk (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom MvPolynomial.C))` (Option A) or
35	an abstract "smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over `k̄` of
36	genus 0" (Option B). The literal Option A is mathematically wrong as
37	written — `Spec(MvPolynomial σ kbar) ⟶ Spec kbar` is the affine, not
38	projective, line — and Mathlib `b80f227` has no `ProjectiveSpace n S`
39	construction packaged as a `Scheme` (`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj` is the
40	general `Proj` of a graded ring, not specialised). The refactor agent
41	took the directive-recommended Option B: the source is a smooth proper
42	geometrically irreducible `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` of relative dimension
43	`1` with `genus C = 0`. This is what the proof decomposition C.2.c–C.2.d
44	actually consume (the keystone uses the curve's `H¹(·, 𝒪) = 0` ↔
45	`genus = 0` characterisation, not a literal `ℙ¹` API).
46	-/
47	
48	set_option autoImplicit false
49	
50	universe u
51	
52	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
53	
54	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
55	
56	variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar]
57	
58	/-- Rigidity for morphisms `C ⟶ A` from a genus-`0` curve over an
59	algebraically closed field `k̄` (encoding `ℙ¹_{k̄}` via the abstract
60	smooth-proper-geometrically-irreducible-of-relative-dimension-`1`-with-
61	`genus = 0` characterisation): every morphism from such a curve to a
62	smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme `A` over `k̄`
63	that hits the identity at a `k̄`-rational point is the constant
64	morphism at the identity.
65	
66	This is the keystone classical input for the M2.a sub-step of the
67	genus-0 Albanese-witness argument. Mumford, *Abelian Varieties*,
68	Chapter~II~§4.
69	
70	**Status**: iter-126 scaffold — body is a single `sorry`. The closure
71	(C.2.b reduction via `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen` + C.2.c image-
72	dimension dichotomy + C.2.d cotangent-vanishing keystone) is gated on
73	the shared cotangent-vanishing Mathlib pile (iter-129+). See
74	`blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex`. -/
75	theorem rigidity_over_kbar
76	    [IsAlgClosed kbar] [CharZero kbar]
77	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
78	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
79	    [IsProper C.hom]
80	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
81	    (_hgenus : genus C = 0)
82	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
83	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
84	    (f : C ⟶ A)
85	    (p : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C)
86	    (_hf : p ≫ f = η[A]) :
87	    f = (toUnit C ≫ η[A]) :=
88	  sorry
89	
90	end AlgebraicGeometry
91

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic
7	import Mathlib.RingTheory.Unramified.Basic
8	import Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Basic
9	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme
10	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.FinitePresentation
11	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Smooth
12	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Differentials.Presheaf
13	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Presheaf
14	
15	/-! # Relative Kähler differentials for schemes (presheaf form)
16	
17	This file constructs the **presheaf** of relative Kähler differentials
18	`Ω_{X/S}` of a morphism of schemes `f : X → S` and states the
19	characterisation of smoothness in terms of pointwise local freeness of
20	`Ω` on affine opens.
21	
22	The construction builds on Mathlib's ring-theoretic `KaehlerDifferential`
23	and the presheaf-of-modules differential construction
24	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`.
25	
26	## References
27	
28	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
38	
39	variable {X S : Scheme.{u}}
40	
41	/-- The relative cotangent **presheaf** of a morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ S`.
42	
43	On each open `U ⊆ X`, the sections are the Kähler differential module of
44	the ring map `O_S(f(U)) → O_X(U)` induced by `f`. More precisely, we use
45	the inverse-image presheaf `f⁻¹ O_S` on `X` and the canonical map to `O_X`;
46	the Kähler differential construction then gives a presheaf of `O_X`-modules.
47	
48	Mathlib leverage: `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` for `f⁻¹`,
49	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`
50	for the presheaf of Kähler differentials. -/
51	noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules :=
52	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
53	    f.c
54	  PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'
55	
56	/-- Localisation-compatibility identity: on each open `V ⊆ X`, the sections
57	of `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f` over `V` are *definitionally* the
58	Kähler differential module of the ring map
59	`(f⁻¹ O_S)(V) → O_X(V)`. -/
60	theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler (f : X ⟶ S)
61	    (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
62	    ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj V : Type _) =
63	      CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential
64	        (((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat
65	          f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c |>.app V) :=
66	  rfl
67	
68	/-- The Kähler module of a localization is subsingleton.
69	This is project-local Lemma `lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton`. -/
70	theorem kaehler_localization_subsingleton
71	    {A L : Type u} [CommRing A] [CommRing L] [Algebra A L]
72	    (M : Submonoid A) [IsLocalization M L] :
73	    Subsingleton (Ω[L⁄A]) :=
74	  letI : Algebra.FormallyUnramified A L :=
75	    Algebra.FormallyUnramified.of_isLocalization (R := A) (Rₘ := L) M
76	  Algebra.FormallyUnramified.subsingleton_kaehlerDifferential
77	
78	/-- Tower-cancellation `LinearEquiv` for the Kähler module under a localization:
79	if `A → L` is a localization at `M ⊆ A` and `L → B` is a ring map forming a
80	scalar tower `A → L → B`, then the canonical map `Ω[B/A] → Ω[B/L]` is a
81	`B`-linear equivalence.
82	
83	This is project-local Lemma `lem:kaehler_quotient_localization_iso` and the most
84	extractable Mathlib contribution candidate of milestone M1 (candidate name
85	`KaehlerDifferential.equivOfFormallyUnramified`). -/
86	noncomputable def kaehler_quotient_localization_iso
87	    {A L B : Type u} [CommRing A] [CommRing L] [CommRing B]
88	    [Algebra A L] [Algebra A B] [Algebra L B] [IsScalarTower A L B]
89	    (M : Submonoid A) [IsLocalization M L] :
90	    Ω[B⁄A] ≃ₗ[B] Ω[B⁄L] := by
91	  haveI : Subsingleton (Ω[L⁄A]) := kaehler_localization_subsingleton M
92	  refine LinearEquiv.ofBijective (KaehlerDifferential.map A L B B) ⟨?_, ?_⟩
93	  · rw [injective_iff_map_eq_zero]
94	    intro x hx
95	    obtain ⟨y, rfl⟩ :=
96	      (KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map A L B x).mp hx
97	    -- y : B ⊗[L] Ω[L⁄A]; since Ω[L⁄A] is subsingleton, the tensor product is too
98	    haveI : Subsingleton (TensorProduct L B (Ω[L⁄A])) := by
99	      refine ⟨fun a b => ?_⟩
100	      have hzero : ∀ z : TensorProduct L B (Ω[L⁄A]), z = 0 := by
101	        intro z
102	        induction z with
103	        | zero => rfl
104	        | tmul x y =>
105	          rw [show y = (0 : Ω[L⁄A]) from Subsingleton.elim _ _, TensorProduct.tmul_zero]
106	        | add x y hx hy => rw [hx, hy, add_zero]
107	      rw [hzero a, hzero b]
108	    rw [Subsingleton.elim y 0, map_zero]
109	  · exact KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective _ _ _
110	
111	/-- Forward direction of the Jacobian criterion (algebra-Kähler form).
112	If `f : X → S` is smooth of relative dimension `n`, then for every
113	point `x ∈ X` there exist affine opens `U ⊆ S` and `V ⊆ X` with
114	`f V ⊆ U` and `x ∈ V`, on which the Kähler differential module
115	`Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]` (over the appLE algebra structure) is a free
116	module of rank `n` over `Γ(X, V)`.
117	
118	The reverse direction (locally free Ω of rank `n` implies smooth of
119	relative dimension `n`) is **mathematically false** as stated without
120	additional deformation-theoretic input (vanishing of
121	`Algebra.H1Cotangent A B` on each chart); see the chapter
122	`Differentials.tex` for the counterexample (`Spec k → Spec k[t]`,
123	`t ↦ 0`) and the converse-direction disclosure. -/
124	theorem smooth_locally_free_omega {n : ℕ} (f : X ⟶ S)
125	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n f] :
126	    ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : S.Opens) (V : X.Opens) (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
127	        x ∈ V ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧ IsAffineOpen V ∧
128	          letI : Algebra Γ(S, U) Γ(X, V) :=
129	            (Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e).hom.toAlgebra
130	          (Module.Free Γ(X, V) (Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]) ∧
131	           Module.rank Γ(X, V) (Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]) = n) := by
132	  intro x
133	  obtain ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hxV, e, hRing⟩ :=
134	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension.exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension (n := n) (f := f) x
135	  refine ⟨U, V, e, hxV, hU, hV, ?_, ?_⟩ <;>
136	    · algebraize [CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e)]
137	      haveI : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth Γ(S, U) Γ(X, V) :=
138	        Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth n
139	      haveI : Nonempty V := ⟨⟨x, hxV⟩⟩
140	      first
141	      | exact Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential
142	      | exact Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential n
143	
144	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
145

14	/-!
15	# Cotangent space at the identity of a group scheme
16	
17	The cotangent space at the identity of a smooth proper geometrically
18	irreducible group scheme `G` over a base field `k`, defined as a
19	`k`-vector space. Mathematically, this is the pullback of the relative
20	cotangent sheaf `Ω_{G/k}` along the identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`;
21	the iter-130 Lean realisation (Replacement (B), per
22	`analogies/lieAlgebra-rank-bridge.md`) computes it as the base change to
23	`k` of the algebraic Kähler module `Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` on a
24	smooth affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around the identity-section image.
25	
26	This is piece (i.a) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile (see
27	`blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` § "Piece (i): sub-lemma
28	decomposition for iter-128+ build" and STRATEGY.md § M2.body-pile).
29	
30	This file contains the definition `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` below, the
31	structural-shape acceptance lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars`
32	below, and the companion rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq`
33	below (rank = relative dimension `n` from
34	`[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]`, closed iter-132).
35	
36	## Status (iter-132 close: rank lemma)
37	
38	`AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` is fully constructed
39	(no `sorry`). The iter-128 body (presheaf evaluated at the top open then
40	extended scalars to `k`) was found in iter-129 to compute the zero
41	`k`-module for every `G` in the target class with `n ≥ 1` (`Spec` of a
42	field is a single point, so the pulled-back presheaf collapses to `k`
43	and the Kähler module of the identity `k → k` is zero). The iter-130
44	fix-up replaced it with **Replacement (B): affine-chart base change** —
45	extract an affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around the identity-section image
46	on which the algebraic Kähler module is free of rank `n` (via
47	`Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`), then base-change to `k` along the
48	identity section restricted to `V`. The iter-130 realisation passed
49	through `Classical.choice` to bridge `Prop` and `Type`, which left the
50	elaborated body with `Classical.choice` as its outermost head symbol —
51	opaque to downstream `unfold`/`whnf`. The **iter-131 fix-up** refactors
52	the body to a pure-term `noncomputable def`: `let`-bindings of
53	`Classical.choose`/`.choose_spec` on the `smooth_locally_free_omega`
54	existential expose `U, V, e, ψV` as named-but-opaque accessors, while
55	the outer expression is the explicit
56	`(ModuleCat.extendScalars ψV.hom).obj (ModuleCat.of … Ω[…])`. The
57	signature, set in iter-129 (free `{n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]`
58	binder), is preserved. The iter-131 body shape is witnessed by
59	`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars` below.
60	
61	The **iter-132 prover lane** closed the companion rank lemma
62	`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (line 244 below) against the
63	iter-131 body via a parallel `Classical.choose`-chain extraction on
64	`Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`, reducing through
65	`Module.finrank_baseChange` + `Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq` to pin the
66	`k`-finrank of the cotangent space at the identity to the relative
67	dimension `n` from the `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]` instance.
68	
69	## References
70	
71	- Blueprint chapter: `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` § "Piece (i)".
72	- Analogist source-of-truth: `analogies/lieAlgebra-rank-bridge.md`
73	  (Replacement (B) construction; iter-130 prover lane closure chain).
74	- Project infrastructure consumed:
75	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`
76	  (in `AlgebraicJacobian.Differentials`).
77	- Mathlib `GrpObj` API: `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp_`,

79	- Mathlib Kähler / standard-smooth pieces (consumed via

82	  `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential`.
83	-/

85	set_option autoImplicit false

87	universe u
88	
89	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
90	open TopologicalSpace Opposite
