586	/-- Iter-045: LinearEquiv between the global-sections module
587	`(Sheaf.Γ J _).obj F` (an object of `ModuleCat k`) and the underlying carrier
588	of `F.obj.obj (op ⊤)` for any sheaf `F` on a topological space `X`.
589	
590	The underlying iso comes from `Sheaf.ΓNatIsoSheafSections` (Mathlib
591	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/GlobalSections.lean`): on a site with terminal
592	`T`, the global-sections functor is naturally iso to evaluation at `T`. For
593	the topology of opens `Opens.grothendieckTopology X`, the terminal in
594	`TopologicalSpace.Opens X` is the top open `⊤` (this is `Preorder.isTerminalTop`
595	for any preorder with a top element). The categorical iso in `ModuleCat k` is
596	converted to a `LinearEquiv` via `Iso.toLinearEquiv` (Mathlib's standard
597	upgrading of `ModuleCat`-isos to LinearEquivs).
598	
599	Iter-046+ uses this `LinearEquiv` together with the linearised constant-sheaf
600	/ global-sections adjunction (multi-iteration; project-local lift of
601	Mathlib's `Adjunction.homAddEquiv` to `≃ₗ[k]`) to construct the producer
602	instance `IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`. -/
603	noncomputable def SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top
604	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {X : TopCat.{u}}
605	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
606	    (Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj F
607	      ≃ₗ[k] F.obj.obj (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)) :=
608	  ((Sheaf.ΓNatIsoSheafSections (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
609	      (ModuleCat.{u} k) (T := ⊤) (Preorder.isTerminalTop _)).app F).toLinearEquiv
610	
611	/-- Iter-045 immediate consumer: combining iter-044's geometric Stein input
612	`module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper` with `SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top`
613	yields `Module.Finite k ((Sheaf.Γ).obj (toModuleKSheaf C))` for a proper
614	integral `Spec k`-scheme `C`.
615	
616	The `haveI` is necessary because `Module.Finite.equiv` does not
617	auto-synthesise the `[Module.Finite k]` hypothesis on the source: the iter-044
618	declaration's conclusion is `Module.Finite k (C.left.presheaf.obj (op ⊤))`,
619	but the source of `(SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top _ _).symm` is
620	`(toModuleKSheaf C).obj.obj (op ⊤)` — these are *the same Module* (via the
621	iter-006 `toModuleKPresheaf_obj` simp lemma) but Lean needs the `haveI` to
622	register the typeclass under the new spelling.
623	
624	This is the algebraic input for the iter-046+ producer-instance assembly:
625	bridging from `Sheaf.Γ.obj` to the Hom group `((constantSheaf @ unit) ⟶ -)`
626	requires the linearised constant-sheaf-Γ adjunction, which is multi-iteration
627	project-local infrastructure. -/
628	theorem module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper
629	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
630	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
631	    Module.Finite k
632	      ((Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
633	        (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)) := by
634	  haveI : Module.Finite k
635	      ((Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C).obj.obj
636	        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)) : ModuleCat k) :=
637	    module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper k C
638	  exact Module.Finite.equiv
639	    (SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)).symm
640	
641	/-- Iter-046: applied LinearEquiv from the constant-sheaf-Γ adjunction. For any
642	sheaf `F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)` and `X : ModuleCat k`, gives a `k`-LinearEquiv
643	between the Hom group `((constantSheaf).obj X ⟶ F)` (sheaf morphisms from the
644	constant sheaf at `X`) and `(X ⟶ (Sheaf.Γ).obj F)` (module morphisms into the
645	global sections of `F`).
646	
647	Built from `(constantSheafΓAdj).homLinearEquiv` (iter-046 Mathlib gap-fill).
648	The five `haveI` lines establish the typeclass scaffolding required to invoke
649	the gap-fill: `presheafToSheaf` Linear (via `sheafificationAdjunction`),
650	`constantSheaf` Additive + Linear (via composition), and `Sheaf.Γ` Additive +
651	Linear (via the `right_adjoint_*` propagators along `constantSheafΓAdj`).
652	
653	Used in iter-046's producer instance `instIsHModuleHomFinite_toModuleKSheaf`
654	to bridge from `Sheaf.Γ.obj`-finiteness (iter-045) to Hom-from-constantSheaf-
655	finiteness (the `IsHModuleHomFinite` carrier). -/
656	noncomputable def constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv
657	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C]
658	    (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
659	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
660	    (X : ModuleCat.{u} k) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
661	    ((constantSheaf J _).obj X ⟶ F) ≃ₗ[k] (X ⟶ (Sheaf.Γ J _).obj F) :=
662	  haveI : (presheafToSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k :=
663	    (sheafificationAdjunction J _).left_adjoint_linear k
664	  haveI : (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Additive := by
665	    unfold constantSheaf; infer_instance
666	  haveI : (Sheaf.Γ J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Additive :=
667	    (constantSheafΓAdj J _).right_adjoint_additive
668	  haveI : (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k := by
669	    unfold constantSheaf; infer_instance
670	  haveI : (Sheaf.Γ J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k :=
671	    (constantSheafΓAdj J _).right_adjoint_linear k
672	  (constantSheafΓAdj J _).homLinearEquiv k X F
673	
674	/-- Iter-046: Hom-from-`k` upgrade. The Hom group `(ModuleCat.of k k ⟶ M)` for
675	`M : ModuleCat k` is canonically `k`-LinearEquivalent to `M` via `f ↦ f 1`.
676	Direct one-liner combining `ModuleCat.homLinearEquiv` (Mathlib's
677	LinearEquiv-version of the underlying-LinearMap correspondence) with
678	`LinearMap.ringLmapEquivSelf` (the standard `(R →ₗ[R] M) ≃ₗ[S] M` evaluation). -/
679	noncomputable def homFromOne_linearEquiv (k : Type u) [Field k] (M : ModuleCat.{u} k) :
680	    (ModuleCat.of k k ⟶ M) ≃ₗ[k] M :=
681	  (ModuleCat.homLinearEquiv (M := ModuleCat.of k k) (N := M) (S := k)).trans
682	    (LinearMap.ringLmapEquivSelf k k M)
683	
684	/-- Iter-046: **the producer instance** for `IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`
685	on a proper integral `Spec k`-scheme `C`. Closes the four-step chain:
686	
687	  (1) `constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv` (iter-046 step 1) bridges the Hom group
688	      `((constantSheaf).obj k ⟶ toModuleKSheaf C)` to `(k ⟶ Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C))`.
689	  (2) `homFromOne_linearEquiv` (iter-046 step 3) identifies `(k ⟶ M)` with `M`
690	      as `k`-modules.
691	  (3) Combined LinearEquiv from (1)+(2): `((constantSheaf).obj k ⟶ toModuleKSheaf C)
692	      ≃ₗ[k] Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C)`.
693	  (4) `module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper` (iter-045 step 2) provides
694	      `Module.Finite k (Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C))` from `[IsIntegral C.left]
695	      [IsProper C.hom]` (iter-044 geometric Stein input).
696	  (5) Transport via `Module.Finite.equiv (.symm)` of the combined LinearEquiv.
697	
698	Once landed, iter-043's curve consumer `module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite_curve`
699	fires automatically on `Module.Finite k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0)` queries,
700	closing the H⁰ side of the genus-finrank Module.Finite ladder.
701	
702	Marked `instance` (not `theorem`) — this is a *producer* of a typeclass instance,
703	to be picked up by typeclass synthesis when the consumer asks for
704	`IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`. Hypotheses `[IsIntegral C.left]`,
705	`[IsProper C.hom]` are class arguments propagated via instance synthesis from
706	the use site (where `C` is concretely a proper integral `Spec k`-scheme, e.g.\ a
707	smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve). -/
708	noncomputable instance instIsHModuleHomFinite_toModuleKSheaf
709	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
710	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
711	    IsHModuleHomFinite k C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) where
712	  module_finite_hom := by
713	    haveI := Scheme.module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper k C
714	    let LE1 := constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv k
715	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.of k k)
716	      (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)
717	    let LE2 := homFromOne_linearEquiv k
718	      ((Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
719	        (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))
720	    exact Module.Finite.equiv (LE1.trans LE2).symm
721	
722	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
723	
724	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
725	
726	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-012 scaffold): the Čech cochain complex of
727	the structure sheaf of a `Spec k`-scheme `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))`,
728	with respect to an arbitrary indexed family of opens `𝒰 : ι → Opens C.left.toTopCat`.
729	Built from Mathlib's `CategoryTheory.cechComplexFunctor` (file
730	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean`) applied to the
731	underlying presheaf of `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` (iter-006). The result is a
732	cochain complex valued in `ModuleCat.{u} k`, indexed by `ℕ`.
733	
734	The cohomology of this complex is `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC` below. The
735	downstream comparison theorem (Čech cohomology = derived-functor cohomology
736	= `Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)` for an acyclic cover) is
737	queued for iter-013+; iter-012 only establishes the Čech-side carrier. -/
738	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCochain_OC
739	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
740	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
741	    CochainComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k) ℕ :=
742	  (cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))
743	
744	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-012 scaffold): the `n`-th Čech cohomology
745	of the structure sheaf for an arbitrary indexed open cover. Defined as the
746	`n`-th homology of the Čech cochain complex `Scheme.cechCochain_OC`. The
747	result lives in `ModuleCat.{u} k` and therefore carries a `Module k`
748	structure for free; the iter-013+ comparison theorem will identify it
749	with `Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n` when the cover is
750	acyclic. -/
751	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCohomology_OC
752	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
753	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ℕ) :
754	    ModuleCat.{u} k :=
755	  (Scheme.cechCochain_OC C 𝒰).homology n
756	
757	/-- Iter-047: parameterised Čech cochain complex generalising iter-012's
758	`Scheme.cechCochain_OC` to any sheaf of `k`-modules `F`, not just the structure
759	sheaf. Built from the same Mathlib `CategoryTheory.cechComplexFunctor`
760	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean`) applied to the
761	underlying presheaf of `F`. The result is a cochain complex valued in
762	`ModuleCat.{u} k`, indexed by `ℕ`. Iter-012's specialisation
763	`Scheme.cechCochain_OC C 𝒰` is recovered by setting `F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`
764	(see `Scheme.cechCochain_OC_eq` below).
765	
766	This generalisation is the foundational scaffolding the iter-048+ Čech-vs-derived
767	comparison theorem will build on: the comparison map
768	`Scheme.cechCohomology k C F 𝒰 n →ₗ[k] Scheme.HModule' k F n (⨆ᵢ 𝒰 i)`
769	(queued for iter-048) is naturally parameterised over the sheaf `F`, not just
770	the structure sheaf, so the parameterised carrier is the right level of
771	generality. -/
772	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCochain
773	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
774	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
775	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
776	    CochainComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k) ℕ :=
777	  (cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj F)
778	
779	/-- Iter-047: parameterised Čech cohomology generalising iter-012's
780	`Scheme.cechCohomology_OC`. The `n`-th cohomology of the parameterised Čech
781	cochain complex. The result lives in `ModuleCat.{u} k`. Iter-012's
782	specialisation is recovered by `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC_eq` below. -/
783	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCohomology
784	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
785	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
786	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ℕ) :
787	    ModuleCat.{u} k :=
788	  (Scheme.cechCochain C F 𝒰).homology n
789	
790	/-- Iter-047 bridge: iter-012's `Scheme.cechCochain_OC` is definitionally the
791	`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` specialisation of `Scheme.cechCochain`. The
792	proof is `rfl` since iter-012's body is the same `(cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj
793	((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))` term. Used by downstream
794	consumers (iter-048+) to switch between the iter-012 structure-sheaf-specific
795	form and the iter-047 parameterised form without semantic loss. -/
796	theorem Scheme.cechCochain_OC_eq
797	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
798	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
799	    Scheme.cechCochain_OC C 𝒰 = Scheme.cechCochain C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰 :=
800	  rfl
801	
802	/-- Iter-047 bridge: iter-012's `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC` is definitionally the
803	`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` specialisation of `Scheme.cechCohomology`. -/
804	theorem Scheme.cechCohomology_OC_eq
805	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
806	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ℕ) :
807	    Scheme.cechCohomology_OC C 𝒰 n =
808	      Scheme.cechCohomology C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰 n :=
809	  rfl
810	
811	/-- Iter-048: Čech-side acyclicity carrier predicate. The cover `𝒰` is
812	*Čech-acyclic for the sheaf `F` of `k`-modules* on `C` if positive-degree Čech
813	cohomology vanishes (in the `Subsingleton` sense — `Scheme.cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n`
814	has type `ModuleCat.{u} k`, but `Subsingleton` on the underlying type is the
815	natural and more chainable form). Mirrors the iter-040 / iter-043 carrier-
816	predicate pattern: a single-field `Prop` class capturing a combinatorial
817	vanishing condition that downstream consumers receive as an instance argument.
818	
819	This is the foundational Čech-side input that the iter-051 producer instance for
820	`IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` will consume, in conjunction
821	with the iter-049+ Čech-vs-derived comparison theorem and iter-048's consumer. -/
822	class Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover
823	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
824	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
825	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) : Prop where
826	  subsingleton_cechCohomology :
827	    ∀ (n : ℕ), 0 < n → Subsingleton (Scheme.cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n)
828	
829	/-- Iter-048: subsingleton transport via Čech acyclicity + comparison.
830	
831	Given the iter-048 carrier hypothesis `[IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰]` AND an explicit
832	comparison iso `compIso n : cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n ≃ₗ[k] HModule' k F n (⨆ 𝒰 i)`
833	(the Čech-vs-derived comparison, queued for iter-049+ to construct as a
834	theorem), conclude that `Subsingleton (HModule' k F n (⨆ 𝒰 i))` for `n ≥ 1`.
835	
836	The `compIso` is taken as an *explicit argument*, not a class field. The
837	comparison itself is a `LinearEquiv` (data), so it cannot be a field of a
838	`Prop`-valued class; more importantly, decoupling the Čech-side combinatorial
839	vanishing (iter-048) from the substantive comparison theorem (iter-049+) lets
840	each step land as a single iteration. Iter-049+ will provide the comparison as
841	a theorem, which iter-048's consumer accepts directly via this argument.
842	
843	The consumer body extracts the class field (the `Subsingleton` on
844	`cechCohomology n`) and transports along `(compIso n).symm.toEquiv.subsingleton`. -/
845	theorem Scheme.subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover
846	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
847	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
848	    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
849	    [Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰]
850	    (compIso : ∀ (n : ℕ),
851	      Scheme.cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n ≃ₗ[k]
852	        Scheme.HModule' k F n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i))
853	    (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) :
854	    Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i)) := by
855	  haveI := Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover.subsingleton_cechCohomology
856	    (F := F) (𝒰 := 𝒰) n hn
857	  exact (compIso n).symm.toEquiv.subsingleton
858	
859	/-- Iter-048: curve specialisation at `F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`.
860	
861	Mirrors the iter-039 / iter-042 / iter-043 `_curve` pattern: a thin dot-notation
862	wrapper that saves call sites in the curve setting (where `F` is the structure
863	sheaf) from re-typing `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` whenever the iter-048 consumer
864	is chained through. Used by the iter-051 `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`
865	producer instance. -/
866	theorem Scheme.subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover_curve
867	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
868	    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
869	    [Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰]
870	    (compIso : ∀ (n : ℕ),
871	      Scheme.cechCohomology C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰 n ≃ₗ[k]
872	        Scheme.HModule' k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i))
873	    (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) :
874	    Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i)) :=
875	  Scheme.subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover (𝒰 := 𝒰) compIso n hn
876	
877	end AlgebraicGeometry

9	/-!
10	# Mayer-Vietoris LES core for `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology
11	
12	This file (iter-063 split, cohort 1) contains the MV LES core for
13	`Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`-valued cohomology: the Mathlib gap-fill
14	`Abelian.Ext.chgUnivLinearEquiv` plus the iter-016 → iter-026 abstract
15	Mayer-Vietoris infrastructure (`HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor`,
16	`HModule'_toBiprod`, `HModule'_fromBiprod`, `HModule'_δ`, `HModule'_sequence`,
17	`HModule'_sequence_exact`, and the `δ`-zero simp companions).
18	
19	This is cohort 1 of the original `MayerVietoris.lean` split:
20	* `MayerVietorisCore.lean` (this file): MV LES core + Mathlib gap-fill.
21	* `MayerVietorisCover.lean`: 2-affine cover MV + cover-totality bridges +
22	  `IsCechAcyclicCover` consumers + `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` carrier.
23	
24	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex`.
25	-/
26	
27	set_option autoImplicit false
28	
29	universe u v w w'
30	
31	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
32	
33	/-! ## Iter-034 Mathlib gap-fill: `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv` as a `LinearEquiv`
34	
35	Mathlib's `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv : Ext.{w} X Y n ≃ Ext.{w'} X Y n`
36	(`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Basic.lean` L540) is a bare
37	`Equiv` only. The iter-034 universe-bridge `HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv`
38	(below in `section CoverTotality`) requires it as a `LinearEquiv` over the
39	`Linear R C`-enriched abelian category. The upgrade is mechanical: the
40	`AddCommGroup (Ext X Y n)` and `Module R (Ext X Y n)` instances are themselves
41	transferred from the standard derived category through `homEquiv`, and
42	Mathlib's load-bearing intermediate lemma `Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv` (Basic.lean
43	L543–545) shows that `chgUniv` preserves the underlying derived-category
44	morphism. So `chgUniv` automatically commutes with `+` and `•`. Two short
45	helper lemmas (`chgUniv_add` and `chgUniv_smul`) plus a 7-line `LinearEquiv`
46	structure literal give the upgrade.
47	
48	This cohort lives outside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme` because the
49	helpers belong to the `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext` namespace. We use the
50	qualified-name pattern `Abelian.Ext.foo` rather than wrapping the entire file
51	in a second namespace block. -/
52	
53	/-- Iter-034 Mathlib gap-fill (a): `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv` is additive. The
54	underlying `homEquiv` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Basic.lean`
55	L162) is preserved by `chgUniv` (Mathlib lemma `Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv`,
56	L543–545), and the `AddCommGroup (Ext X Y n)` instance (L241–243) is defined by
57	transferring the `AddCommGroup` from the standard derived category through
58	`homEquiv`. Combining these via the `Ext.ext` extensionality (L177–179) gives
59	additivity. -/
60	private lemma Abelian.Ext.chgUniv_add
61	    {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C]
62	    [HasExt.{w} C] [HasExt.{w'} C] {X Y : C} {n : ℕ} (a b : Abelian.Ext.{w} X Y n) :
63	    Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} (a + b) =
64	      Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a + Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} b := by
65	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
66	  ext
67	  rw [Abelian.Ext.add_hom, ← Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply,
68	      ← Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply, ← Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply]
69	  change Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} (a + b)) =
70	    Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a) +
71	      Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} b)
72	  rw [Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv, Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv,
73	      Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv, ← Abelian.Ext.add_hom]
74	
75	/-- Iter-034 Mathlib gap-fill (b): `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv` is `R`-linear when `C`
76	is `Linear R`-enriched. Same proof shape as `chgUniv_add`, but using
77	`smul_hom` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Linear.lean` L52–55)
78	in place of `add_hom`. -/
79	private lemma Abelian.Ext.chgUniv_smul
80	    {R : Type*} [Ring R] {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C] [Linear R C]
81	    [HasExt.{w} C] [HasExt.{w'} C] {X Y : C} {n : ℕ}
82	    (r : R) (a : Abelian.Ext.{w} X Y n) :
83	    Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} (r • a) = r • Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a := by
84	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
85	  ext
86	  rw [Abelian.Ext.smul_hom, ← Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply,
87	      ← Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply]
88	  change Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} (r • a)) =
89	    r • Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a)
90	  rw [Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv, Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv,
91	      ← Abelian.Ext.smul_hom]
92	
93	/-- Iter-034 Mathlib gap-fill (c): the `R`-linear upgrade of Mathlib's bare-`Equiv`
94	`Abelian.Ext.chgUniv : Ext.{w} X Y n ≃ Ext.{w'} X Y n` to a `LinearEquiv`,
95	when `C` is `Linear R`-enriched abelian. Combines (a) and (b). The
96	`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Basic.lean` L540 declaration is
97	universe-polymorphic but only proves the underlying bijection; this wrapper
98	adds the algebraic structure preservation needed for downstream `LinearEquiv`
99	chaining (specifically, the iter-034 universe bridge
100	`HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv` below). -/

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.MayerVietorisCore
7	
8	/-!
9	# Mayer-Vietoris on a 2-affine cover and Čech-acyclic cover infrastructure
10	
11	This file (iter-063 split, cohort 2) contains the 2-affine cover
12	Mayer-Vietoris theorems, the cover-totality bridges (universe-shift
13	`LinearEquiv`s, `finrank` / `Module.Finite` transports), the
14	`IsCechAcyclicCover` consumers, the comparison-iso typeclass
15	`HasCechToHModuleIso`, and the existence-bundled
16	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` predicate together with the producer that
17	fires `IsAffineHModuleVanishing` from a `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover`
18	hypothesis.
19	
20	This is cohort 2 of the original `MayerVietoris.lean` split:
21	* `MayerVietorisCore.lean`: MV LES core + Mathlib gap-fill.
22	* `MayerVietorisCover.lean` (this file): 2-affine cover MV +
23	  cover-totality bridges + `IsCechAcyclicCover` consumers +
24	  `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` carrier.
25	
26	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex`.
27	-/
28	
29	set_option autoImplicit false
30	
31	universe u v w w'
32	
33	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
36	
37	section AffineCoverMVSquare
38	
39	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-028): a bundled 2-affine cover of a scheme
40	together with the affineness of the pairwise intersection. The geometric input
41	to the abstract Mayer-Vietoris LES `HModule'_sequence_exact` consumes — packaged
42	to record the affineness conditions and the cover-totality hypothesis that the
43	abstract MV-square does not capture. The accessor `toMayerVietorisSquare`
44	produces the underlying Mathlib `MayerVietorisSquare`.
45	
46	Mathematically: a 2-affine open cover of `X` whose pairwise intersection is also
47	affine. The cover hypothesis `cover : U₁ ⊔ U₂ = ⊤` is load-bearing for downstream
48	Serre-finiteness use (it identifies the `MayerVietorisSquare` corner `X₄` with
49	the whole scheme `X`). -/
50	structure AffineCoverMVSquare (X : Scheme.{u}) where
51	  /-- First affine open of the cover. -/
52	  U₁ : X.Opens
53	  /-- Second affine open of the cover. -/
54	  U₂ : X.Opens
55	  /-- `U₁` is affine. -/
56	  isAffineOpen_U₁ : IsAffineOpen U₁
57	  /-- `U₂` is affine. -/
58	  isAffineOpen_U₂ : IsAffineOpen U₂
59	  /-- The intersection `U₁ ⊓ U₂` is affine. -/
60	  isAffineOpen_inf : IsAffineOpen (U₁ ⊓ U₂)
61	  /-- Total cover: `U₁ ⊔ U₂ = ⊤`. -/
62	  cover : U₁ ⊔ U₂ = ⊤
63	
64	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-028): the underlying abstract Mayer-Vietoris
65	square produced by a 2-affine cover. Bridge from the geometric input (an
66	`AffineCoverMVSquare`) to the categorical input (a `MayerVietorisSquare` for
67	the Grothendieck topology on `Opens X.toTopCat`) the abstract MV-LES theorem
68	`HModule'_sequence_exact` consumes. The four corners collapse definitionally:
69	`X₁ = U₁ ⊓ U₂`, `X₂ = U₁`, `X₃ = U₂`, `X₄ = U₁ ⊔ U₂` (each by `rfl`,
70	plan-agent probe-verified). -/
71	noncomputable def AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare {X : Scheme.{u}}
72	    (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
73	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat).MayerVietorisSquare :=
74	  Opens.mayerVietorisSquare S.U₁ S.U₂
75	
76	/-- Iter-029 corner identification: `X₁ = U₁ ⊓ U₂`. The first three corners are
77	`rfl`-equal because `Opens.mayerVietorisSquare` defines them definitionally. -/
78	@[simp]
79	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X₁
80	    {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
81	    S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X₁ = S.U₁ ⊓ S.U₂ := rfl
82	
83	/-- Iter-029 corner identification: `X₂ = U₁`. -/
84	@[simp]
85	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X₂
86	    {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
87	    S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X₂ = S.U₁ := rfl
88	
89	/-- Iter-029 corner identification: `X₃ = U₂`. -/
90	@[simp]
91	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X₃
92	    {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
93	    S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X₃ = S.U₂ := rfl
94	
95	/-- Iter-029 cover-totality identification: `X₄ = ⊤`. The substantive corner
96	identification — uses the `cover` field, which is the totality hypothesis
97	`U₁ ⊔ U₂ = ⊤` recorded in `AffineCoverMVSquare`. -/
98	@[simp]
99	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X₄
100	    {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :

108	  right_inv a := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'}.right_inv a

112	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
113	
114	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-016): the substantive functor mirroring
115	Mathlib's `Sheaf.cohomologyPresheafFunctor` for the `ModuleCat k` flavor. Sends
116	a sheaf `F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)` to the presheaf
117	`X ↦ Ext^n((presheafToSheaf J _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ ModuleCat.free k).obj X), F)`,
118	i.e.\ to a presheaf `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` whose value at `op X` is
119	`HModule' k F n X` (definitionally, see `HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf` below).
120	
121	The codomain is `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` (not `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat k`) because Mathlib's
122	`Abelian.extFunctor n : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ C ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` always lands in `AddCommGrpCat`
123	regardless of the source category's `Linear`-enrichment; the per-fiber `Module k`
124	structure on `(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (op X)` is preserved
125	through the iter-014 `HModule'` reducibility chain. Probe-confirmed body
126	(iter-016 plan-agent). Used downstream as the prerequisite for the queued
127	iter-017+ `ModuleCat k`-flavored Mayer-Vietoris LES. -/
128	noncomputable def HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor
129	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
130	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
131	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
132	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
133	    (n : ℕ) :
134	    Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k) ⥤ Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat :=
135	  Functor.flip
136	    ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙
137	      presheafToSheaf _ _).op ⋙ Abelian.extFunctor n)
138	
139	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-016): the abbrev wrapper mirroring Mathlib's
140	`Sheaf.cohomologyPresheaf` for the `ModuleCat k` flavor. Evaluates the
141	`HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor` at a sheaf `F`, giving a presheaf
142	`Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` whose value at `op X` is `HModule' k F n X` definitionally
143	(probe-confirmed `rfl` by the iter-016 plan-agent). The `noncomputable abbrev`
144	form is required for the per-fiber definitional identification with `HModule'`;
145	under `def` the wrapper would block the `rfl`-level reduction that downstream
146	Mayer-Vietoris and Stein-factorization arguments rely on. -/
147	noncomputable abbrev HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf
148	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
149	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
150	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
151	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
152	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
153	    Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat :=
154	  (HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor k J n).obj F
155	
156	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-017): the first Mayer-Vietoris LES building
157	block on the `ModuleCat k` side — the sum of the two restriction maps
158	`(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₂₄.op` and `(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₃₄.op`,
159	as a single map into the biproduct. Direct mirror of Mathlib's
160	`GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.toBiprod` (file
161	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`, L43–46) for
162	the `ModuleCat k` flavor with `F.cohomologyPresheaf → HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F`.
163	
164	The codomain `(... ).obj (op S.X₂) ⊞ (...).obj (op S.X₃)` is the biproduct in
165	`AddCommGrpCat` (Mathlib `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/Grp/Biproducts.lean`); since
166	`HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n` is `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat`, the biproduct
167	auto-resolves. Probe-confirmed body (iter-017 plan-agent). Used downstream as the
168	first of three Mayer-Vietoris LES building blocks; iter-018 will add the
169	composition-is-zero lemma and the connecting hom `δ`, iter-019 the LES sequence
170	and exactness theorem. -/
171	noncomputable def HModule'_toBiprod
172	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
173	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
174	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
175	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
176	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
177	    (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₄) ⟶
178	      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂) ⊞
179	        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃) :=
180	  biprod.lift ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₂₄.op)
181	              ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₃₄.op)
182	
183	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-017): the second Mayer-Vietoris LES building
184	block on the `ModuleCat k` side — the difference of the two restriction maps
185	`(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₁₂.op` and `(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₁₃.op`,
186	as a single map out of the biproduct. Direct mirror of Mathlib's
187	`GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.fromBiprod` (file
188	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`, L67–70) for
189	the `ModuleCat k` flavor.
190	
191	The negation `-(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₃.op` uses the
192	preadditive structure on `AddCommGrpCat`-morphisms; the sign is the standard
193	Mayer-Vietoris convention encoding the alternating-sum structure of the Čech
194	complex. Probe-confirmed body (iter-017 plan-agent). -/
195	noncomputable def HModule'_fromBiprod
196	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
197	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
198	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
